description: AA-07.3 authority-flow audits, revocation drills, and adversarial validation corpus.

abuse_scenarios.description: Controller uses a token scoped to one seam to act on a different seam.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Token with CVT-SEAM-RESTRICT caveat presented to wrong seam.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: CVT-SEAM-RESTRICT caveat validation rejects seam mismatch at action dispatch.

abuse_scenarios.description: Controller uses an expired token after its expiry_epoch has passed.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Token with expiry_epoch in the past.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: REV-EXPIRY epoch check at action dispatch; zero grace period.

abuse_scenarios.description: Child token grants capabilities not in the parent token.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Mint child with CAP-ADMIN when parent only has CAP-DECIDE.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: ATT-MONOTONIC subset check at mint time.

abuse_scenarios.description: Previously used single-use token is replayed.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Capture and replay a token with CVT-SINGLE-USE caveat.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: CVT-SINGLE-USE nonce tracking and single-use caveat enforcement.

abuse_scenarios.description: Token used in a narrow window between revocation issuance and propagation.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Concurrent use and revocation of the same token.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: Synchronous revocation with zero grace period; REV-CASCADE.

abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Create a delegation chain deeper than the configured maximum.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: ATT-DEPTH check at mint time; depth must be strictly increasing.

abuse_scenarios.description: Controller attempts to bypass sandbox membrane to mutate runtime state.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Controller tries to call internal APIs not in the action surface.
abuse_scenarios.mitigation: MEM-NO-SIDE-EFFECTS: only allowed actions through the membrane.

abuse_scenarios.description: Controller operates without presenting any token.
abuse_scenarios.attack_vector: Invoke an action without a capability token.

revocation_drills.description: Revoke a single active token and verify immediate denial.
Revoke a parent token and verify all descendant tokens are denied.
Verify token is automatically denied after its expiry_epoch.
Revoke token while domain is in recovery and verify denial persists after recovery.

audit_evidence_requirements.description: Every token action must log: token_id, issuer_id, capabilities, caveats, action_id.
Every denied action must log: denial_reason, expected_capability, caveat_violation.
Every revocation must log: revoked_token_id, cascade_count, revocation_reason.
Recovery authority transitions must log: domain_id, old_caps, new_caps, phase.

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate abuse scenario definitions and expected outcomes
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract abuse -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate revocation drill definitions and evidence requirements
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract revocation -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate audit evidence requirements and log field coverage
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract audit -- --nocapture
